auth.title: WithYou
auth.subtitle: AI-Driven Mental Health & Productivity Hub for Students
auth.footer: Protected by Firebase Security & Google SSO

profile.title: Configure Your Path
profile.subtitle: WithYou tailors recommendations and stress indicators to your target examination.

assessment.title: Initial Mental Health Assessment
assessment.subtitle: Please set your baseline statistics. The Gemini AI Engine uses this to calculate burnout risks and custom coping strategies.
assessment.stress_mid: Moderate exam pressure, manageable but noticeable.
assessment.stress_high: Experiencing high anxiety, tension or exam burnout.
assessment.sleep_low: Sleep deprived. Cognitive focus and energy may be compromised.
assessment.sleep_mid: Sufficient rest, but try to aim for 7-8 hours.

dashboard.header_sub: Your competitive tracker is synced with your {{exam}} study objectives.

journal.title: Expressive Daily Journal
journal.subtitle: Write down your thoughts or use the Voice Journal. Gemini AI analyzes your underlying stress triggers, confidence levels, and burnout risks.
journal.placeholder: How are you feeling today? What did you study? What is stressing you out? (Type here or record your voice below...)
journal.speak_freely: Speak freely about your exam stress
journal.no_analysis: No active analysis log. Submit a journal entry to unlock cognitive strain and safety insights.
journal.coach_directives: Coach directives compiled! Check the AI Coach Tab.

coach.title: Personalized AI Coach
coach.breathing_sub: Calm your autonomic nervous system using the clinical 4-4-4-4 second breath pattern.

study_plan.title: Personalized AI Study Plan
study_plan.subtitle: A dynamic daily roadmap synchronized with your stress, sleep patterns, and exam syllabus priorities.
study_plan.warning_active: Gemini AI Warning: Burnout Recovery Schedule Active
study_plan.warning_sub: Pacing is optimized with smaller study blocks and expanded recovery intervals to reduce stress.
study_plan.normal_sub: Configured for maximum retention and concentration blocks for your {{exam}} exam.

analytics.title: Progress Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Historical tracking of your biological parameters and mental stress trends.
analytics.correlation_text: Your logs show that whenever sleep falls below 6 hours, your stress spikes by 40% and overall focus declines dramatically.
analytics.burnout_text: You hit a cognitive fatigue peak on Thursday after studying 11 hours straight. Recovery sleep on Friday helped re-align levels.
analytics.ratio_text: A healthy ratio for competitive exams is between 1:1 and 1:1.5. Thursday's ratio of 1:2.2 was inefficient due to fatigue.
analytics.consistency_text: You met your daily study hour target on 5 out of 7 days. Focus on stabilizing your sleep schedule to stay above 80%.

safe_circle.title: SafeCircle Support
safe_circle.subtitle: Reaching out is a sign of strength. Select a trusted contact to auto-generate a conversation starter.
safe_circle.empty_state: Select a family member, friend or mentor to establish a supportive communication bridge.
safe_circle.consent_text: WithYou requires your explicit permission to initiate a simulated {{action}} to {{name}}.

risk.title: Wellness Risk Detection
risk.subtitle: Automated cognitive load and burnout analyzer

high_risk.directive: Urgent Burnout Prevention protocol active. We highly advise immediate pause on preparation.
high_risk.tips_0: Decline mock test submissions for the next 24 hours.
high_risk.tips_1: Schedule an immediate call with a trusted contact in your SafeCircle.
high_risk.tips_2: Shut down all devices and aim for 8+ hours of continuous sleep tonight.

med_risk.directive: Elevated cognitive load and mild sleep debt. Pacing adjustments needed.
med_risk.tips_0: Incorporate a 10-minute active stretch for every 90 minutes of study.
med_risk.tips_1: Complete a Box Breathing session before your next study sprint.
med_risk.tips_2: Ensure study duration does not exceed 8 hours today.
med_risk.action: Proactive study pacer has been adjusted to include 15% more breaks.

low_risk.directive: Excellent preparation alignment. Mental stress is well buffered by sleep.
low_risk.tips_0: Maintain your current sleep schedule of 7.5+ hours.
low_risk.tips_1: Hydrate consistently to keep neural pathways running efficiently.
low_risk.tips_2: Maintain your daily study hour goal with active recall tasks.
low_risk.action: Maintain consistency to achieve peak retention efficiency on exam day.

report.title: Weekly AI Wellness & Prep Report
report.period: Report Period: June 21, 2026 - June 27, 2026 | Compiled by Gemini AI Engine
report.report_desc: This index integrates your sleep hygiene, reported stress peaks, self-confidence indicators, and daily revision consistency over the past 7 days.
report.synthesis_desc: The student exhibits excellent preparation velocity, logging consistent hours. However, biometric mapping detects a tight inverse coupling between sleep hours and daily stress. High cognitive stress on mid-week days was directly driven by mock assessment thresholds.
report.synthesis_recommend: Recommended Adjustment: Limit continuous mock practice to 3-hour focus blocks only.

ai.welcome: Hello {{name}}! I am WithYou, your mental wellness companion. I know you're preparing for the {{exam}} exam. How are you feeling today? I'm here to listen or offer tailored study tips!

coach.motivation_low: You have the capacity to master this. Consistency beats intensity. Take it one question at a time.
coach.motivation_high: Your mental wellness is your actual competitive advantage. Pushing when exhausted yields negative returns. Rest now to study stronger later.
coach.study_low: Focus on solving active pyqs (previous year questions) for 45 minutes, then take a hard 10-minute break.
coach.study_high: Lower the cognitive load: break chapters into micro-topics of 15 minutes. Avoid full mock papers today.
coach.break_low: You've studied long hours. Take a 15-minute mindfulness walk without your phone.
coach.break_high: Immediate study cessation recommended. Lie down, close your eyes, and complete 4 cycles of Box Breathing.
coach.coping_low: Recall that an exam does not define your worth. Ground yourself in the present moment.
coach.coping_high: Anxiety tricks you into feeling unprepared. Write down 3 specific concepts you are comfortable with to restore balance.

chat.stressed_high: Oh, {{name}}... I hear you, and my heart truly goes out to you right now. Preparing for an exam as massive as {{exam}} is a monumental mountain to climb, and the sheer weight of expectations—both your own and those around you—can feel completely suffocating. I can feel the exhaustion and fear in your words, and I want you to hear this clearly: you do not have to carry this crushing burden all by yourself. It is okay to feel scared. It is okay to cry. Your worth as a human being is not, and will never be, defined by an entrance exam. Let's do a simple reset together. Close your eyes, let your shoulders drop, and take three slow, grounding breaths with me. I am right here with you, holding this space. Have you shared how heavy you're feeling with anyone in your SafeCircle today?
chat.stressed_low: I hear the tension in your words, {{name}}, and I want to validate that feeling. It is a completely natural human response because you care deeply about your future. But please remember: you are a human being, not a machine. A mock test score is useless if it costs you your inner peace. Let's break the prep down into tiny, manageable steps so you don't feel swallowed by the whole syllabus. What specific topic is making you feel anxious right now? Let's look at it together, step-by-step.
chat.tired: I can feel how incredibly drained you are, {{name}}. It breaks my heart to think of you pushing your mind and body to this absolute breaking point. Your health is not a trade-off for your preparation—it is the foundation of it. When you are sleep-deprived, your brain can't absorb or recall the very concepts you are working so hard to learn. Rest is not a reward you earn after studying; it is a physiological necessity. Please, close the books for tonight. Turn off your screen. Drink a warm glass of water, wrap yourself in comfort, and let your mind go completely dark. You have done more than enough today. I am right here, and I promise the books will wait. Go sleep, okay?
chat.study: It is so hard to balance everything, isn't it? The syllabus looms so large, and time feels like sand slipping through your fingers. When we get anxious, we try to force ourselves to study out of guilt, but quality always beats empty, exhausted hours. Let's design a schedule that respects your humanity: 50 minutes of focused review, followed by a 10-minute transition break where you stretch, walk, or listen to a calming track. I'd love to help you build this in the AI Study Plan tab, but first, tell me honestly: does your mind feel ready to focus, or does it need a gentle, guilt-free pause first?
chat.quiz: I would love to run a quick mental booster quiz with you, {{name}}, but only if you feel up for it. I want to make sure your brain feels stimulated, not stressed. Here is a gentle scenario: 'If you encounter a highly complex, unfamiliar question on your {{exam}} paper, what is the kindest and most strategic thing to do for your mental state?' A) Stay and fight it for 15 minutes because you feel you have to prove yourself, or B) Bookmark it, take a slow breath, skip it, and secure the easier marks first. Take your time.
chat.quiz_correct: Exactly. Choosing B is a sign of deep academic maturity and self-compassion. You are choosing to protect your confidence rather than letting a single question trigger panic. I am so proud of your strategic alignment!
chat.fallback: I am listening, {{name}}. I want to understand what is happening inside you. Preparation for {{exam}} is a long, lonely marathon, and it is completely normal to feel lost or overwhelmed sometimes. Tell me more about what is on your mind today. I am here to hold space for you, to listen, and to support you, without any judgment whatsoever.

safe_circle.mom_stressed: Hey Mom, study is going okay but I am feeling really overwhelmed and exhausted today. Do you have a few minutes for a quick chat?
safe_circle.mom_calm: Hey Mom! Finished my mock session for {{exam}} today. Just wanted to hear your voice and take a short study break.
safe_circle.dad_stressed: Hey Dad, mock exam prep for {{exam}} is getting pretty intense and I'm feeling stressed about my current scores. Could we talk for a bit? I'd appreciate some advice.
safe_circle.dad_calm: Hey Dad, study goals for today are on track. Just finished my Revision block and wanted to check in.
safe_circle.friend_stressed: Hey man, this syllabus is driving me crazy and I'm feeling super burnt out. You free for a quick tea break or call?
safe_circle.friend_calm: Hey Kabir! Finished today's {{exam}} study blocks. You up for a quick sync later tonight?
safe_circle.mentor: Hello Sir, I finished today's topic coverage for {{exam}} but I'm feeling anxious about my accuracy in mock papers. Do you have 5 minutes to guide me on strategy?

journal.transcript_0: I've been studying for 10 hours today for the exam. My scores aren't improving and I feel extremely overwhelmed. I only slept 4 hours last night and I feel like I'm going to crash.
journal.transcript_1: Today was a better study day, but the sheer volume of syllabus is keeping me awake at night. I feel stressed but I'm trying to push through.
journal.transcript_2: Mock test is in two days. I am scoring around 550 but my target is 650. I feel like my parents are expecting too much and it's putting immense pressure on me.
journal.summary: Student is dedicating significant hours but experiencing emotional strain from study goals.
journal.anxiety_high: Significant stress and cognitive fatigue detected
journal.trigger_ratio: High study hours vs low sleep

morning.title: Morning Mind & Body Routine
morning.subtitle: Your personalized daily wellness routine, adapted to your current state
morning.allDone: Amazing! You've completed your morning routine! Your mind and body are ready.
